Escalation probability HIGH. Supervisor escalation requested.

Betty Martin

Two independent witnesses confirmed at-fault driver ran a red light prior to collision.

text_incident: Claimant Ronald Roberts experienced Fracture on 2021-04-09 when a commercial truck driver, employed by FastHaul Logistics, ran through a red light at high speed and struck claimant's vehicle on the driver's side. The truck driver had two prior DUIs on record and the employer had not conducted a background check per DOT requirements. Federal motor carrier records confirm multiple violations in the 60 days prior.
text_adjuster_notes: CONTACT LOG #1538 | Claimant called at 12:15. Tone: HOSTILE. Stated verbatim: 'I have retained legal counsel and they will be contacting you this week.' Demanded full settlement of claimed amount within 5 business days. Referenced 'bad faith' twice. Previous adjuster notes show this is the 4th escalating contact in 10 days. Supervisor notified. Legal hold placed on file. ACTION: Escalate to litigation unit.
text_email: From: Ronald Roberts <claimant@email.com> To: claims@insurecorp.com Subject: State Insurance Commissioner Complaint Filed — Claim #CLM-2022-10537 Date: 2022-03-08 To Whom It May Concern, Please be advised that I have today filed a formal complaint with the State Department of Insurance regarding the handling of Claim #CLM-2022-10537. I have documented over 202 days of delays, three inconsistent adjuster assessments, and a settlement offer of $42,856 that bears no relation to the $168,529 in actual documented losses. I have retained a public adjuster who has independently verified the damages. I have also recorded my last four phone conversations with your representatives. My attorney is reviewing these recordings. Unless I receive a written commitment to full settlement within five business days, I will pursue every legal remedy available to me, including contacting local media regarding your company's claim handling practices. Ronald Roberts
